content: The built-in Tag feature doesn't provide for entering dates; it's only for applying a color to the message by selecting the color. Each color is numbered. If you did in fact mean to say number instead of date, you can undo the tag by clicking the message in the message list, then press the zero key.
creator: siffemoz

content: That message indicates that the profile has been tampered with or moved from another PC improperly. If you are willing to start over, click to c:\users\<yourid>\appdata\roaming and DELETE the folder named thunderbird. Do this with thunderbird not running. Then restart thunderbird and it should create a baseline profile. If you had transferred from another computer, we can assist, probably, in getting this fixed. Tell us more.

content:
They're probably gone. BUT, you can verify.

  1. click help>troubleshootinginformation
  2. scroll to 'profile folder' and click 'open folder'
  3. if IMAP file, click the imapmail folder. If POP, click the mail folder
  4. now click the account folder where you should see such as inbox, inbox.msf, sent, sent.msf, junk, jun.msf, etc. Is there a file named Trash? There is likely one named Trash.msf, but you're looking for one named Trash.
  5. if you find it, rightclick and open in a simple text editor, such as Notepad. If there is no Trash file, then the trans was somehow emptied. Let me know if you found the Trash folder and we can proceed from there.

content:
It's not clear to me from your description whether you use Thunderbird to interface with your Yahoo mail account, or if you have a separate app for Yahoo, or you use Yahoo web mail.

Yahoo is correct, the "new mail" sound for Thunderbird is local to your computer. Out of the box, Thunderbird will use the default system sound, which might clash with a separate program trying to use that sound at the same time. You can configure Thunderbird to use a different sound. See <Settings | General | Incoming | Play a sound | Use the following sound file...>.

A Yahoo app or web page would have it's own sound or might use the default system sound, which can also be changed in the operating system settings.
creator: rednoise
